_comment: Use Case: Node.js/TypeScript codebases using pre-installed npm scripts.
- node_modules is pre-installed and locked via package-lock.json
- No npm install, yarn add, or pnpm add permitted during sessions
- Scripts run via 'npm run <script>' only — no arbitrary node execution
- Project does not contain inline secrets (enforce via pre-commit + gitleaks)
Copy this file to your project root at .claude/settings.json
This file EXTENDS the base deny-list — it does not replace it.
The managed_settings.json deny-list always takes precedence.
rules: PROJECT CONTEXT: You are assisting with a Node.js/TypeScript project. Your permitted actions are: reading source files, running pre-defined npm scripts for testing, linting, formatting, and type-checking. You must not install, upgrade, or remove packages under any circumstance.
LOCKFILE POLICY: package-lock.json, yarn.lock, and pnpm-lock.yaml are frozen. You must not modify lockfiles or suggest dependency changes without stopping for human review.
BUILD POLICY: You may run the build script to verify compilation, but must not deploy, publish, or push build artifacts without explicit human approval that names the target environment.
